title: Factor of Safety for Infinite Slope Stability
content: For a saturated infinite slope: FS=(c'+(γ−γw)·z·cos²β·tanφ')/(γ·z·sinβ·cosβ), where c' is effective cohesion (kPa), γ and γw are bulk and water unit weights (kN/m³), z is depth to slip plane (m), β is slope angle, and φ' is effective friction angle. FS<1.0 indicates imminent failure; monitoring systems typically issue warnings at FS<1.5. Dynamic triggering from rainfall infiltration rapidly reduces c' and increases γ.
